title: cerinthe aspera subsp. gymnandra
intro: Cerinthe aspera subsp. gymnandra, a subspecies of the common hemp-nettle or honeywort plant, is a perennial plant in the borage family native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Cerinthe aspera subsp. gymnandra lie in its leaves, flowers, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of Cerinthe aspera subsp. gymnandra include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other remedies for various health conditions.
